meta.title: -ist

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Every word and meaning transcribed faithfully; the teaching layer (mentor_note, discovery) is authored for CAT OS.

meta.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Word DNA milestone); WORD_DNA_BIBLE frameworks applied
meta.reviewer_notes: philatelist is taught here (having been held out for transfer in wd-0002, phil) so the two units reinforce each other exactly as the source's own overlap intends. numismatist held out as the obscure transfer test. Owner review pending.

unit.label: -ist
unit.core_meaning: one who performs an action, is an expert in a field, or holds a belief
unit.mentor_note: This one suffix quietly names hundreds of professions. Once you know it, a job title stops being a mystery and starts being a clue.

members.meaning: an expert in or strict adherent to traditions
one who collects or studies stamps
an expert in or collector of coins

predict_options.text: Someone who specializes in or studies something

discovery.understand_note: Ist marks a person defined by their subject: a geneticist studies genetics, an arborist cares for trees, a meteorologist studies weather. Swap in almost any field, and -ist names its expert.

applies.prompt: Knowing -ist marks someone who specializes in something, what do you think a numismatist studies or collects?
